input     : Q: Explain the Python GIL and its impact on multi-threaded programs.
A: The GIL is something in Python that affects threading. It makes multi-threaded programs slower because only one thread can run at a time.
output    : {
  "score": 20,
  "pass": false,
  "strengths": [
    "Identified the basic limitation that the GIL restricts execution to one thread at a time."
  ],
  "weaknesses": [
    "Lacks the technical depth expected from a Senior Software Engineer.",
    "Failed to identify that the GIL is specific to CPython and protects memory management/reference counting.",
    "Did not distinguish between the impact on CPU-bound versus I/O-bound tasks.",
    "Failed to mention workarounds or alternatives like the multiprocessing module."
  ],
  "missing_keywords": [
    "CPython",
    "Mutex",
    "Bytecode",

    "I/O-bound",
    "Reference counting",
    "Memory management",
    "Multiprocessing"
  ],

}

A: The GIL is something in Python that affects threading. It makes multi-threaded programs slower because only one thread can run at a time.
output    : {
  "score": 25,
  "pass": false,
  "strengths": [
    "Correctly identified that the GIL restricts threads to running one at a time"
  ],
  "weaknesses": [
    "Extremely superficial explanation, failing to define the GIL as a mutex specific to CPython",
    "Missed the critical distinction between CPU-bound tasks (which are bottlenecked) and I/O-bound tasks (which still benefit from threading)",
    "Failed to explain why the GIL exists (to protect reference counts and memory management)",
    "Did not mention any workarounds, such as using the multiprocessing module",
    "Lacks the technical depth expected from a Senior Software Engineer"
  ],
